The **New Hampshire [at-large](/wiki/At-large "At-large") congressional district** is obsolete, with representation having since been divided into districts.

However, from 1789 to 1847, [New Hampshire](/wiki/New_Hampshire "New Hampshire") elected members to the [United States House of Representatives](/wiki/United_States_House_of_Representatives "United States House of Representatives") at-large:

* From 1789 to 1793, three members represented the state at-large.
* From 1793 to 1803, four members represented the state at-large.
* From 1803 to 1813, five members represented the state at-large.
* From 1813 to 1833, six members represented the state at-large.
* From 1833 to 1843, five members represented the state at-large.
* From 1843 to 1847, four members represented the state at-large.
* In 1847 at-large representation was replaced by four [congressional districts](/wiki/Congressional_district "Congressional district").
